title: Chiang Mai 5-Day Cultural Tour
metaTitle: Chiang Mai 5-Day Cultural Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Chiang Mai, Thailand: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Wat Phra That Doi Suthep and Elephant Nature Park. Prices from $1573 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Chiang Mai, Thailand — Tropical climate with three seasons — cool and dry (Nov-Feb, highs around 28°C), hot (Mar-May, highs 35°C+), and rainy (Jun-Oct). November to February — the cool, dry season with comfortable temperatures ideal for temple-hopping and trekking. This itinerary blends Wat Phra That Doi Suthep, Elephant Nature Park, Old City temple walk with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Wat Phra That Doi Suthep, Elephant Nature Park, Old City temple walk
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Chiang Mai
itinerary.description: Land at Chiang Mai International Airport (CNX) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Wat Phra That Doi Suthep — Golden hilltop temple overlooking the city, reached by a 300-step staircase or cable car. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Chiang Mai: Elephant Nature Park & Doi Inthanon National Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Elephant Nature Park — Ethical elephant sanctuary focused on rescue and rehabilitation (no riding). In the afternoon, continue to Doi Inthanon National Park — Thailand's highest peak, with waterfalls and hill-tribe villages. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Chiang Mai: Chiang Mai Night Safari & Old City temple walk
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Chiang Mai Night Safari — Nighttime zoo tram tour. In the afternoon, continue to Old City temple walk — Wat Chedi Luang, Wat Phra Singh, and dozens more within the moated old town. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Doi Inthanon National Park
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Doi Inthanon National Park, a popular side trip from Chiang Mai. Return to Chiang Mai in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Doi Inthanon National Park

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Sunday Walking Street market or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to CNX for your departure flight.

travelTips: Visit Wat Phra That Doi Suthep early morning to avoid both heat and tour-bus crowds.
Choose an ethical, no-riding elephant sanctuary like Elephant Nature Park.
Book Yi Peng lantern festival tickets months ahead if visiting in November — it sells out.
Use Grab rather than street-hailing a songthaew if unsure of fair pricing.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Layers — cool-season mornings and evenings can be surprisingly chilly
Comfortable shoes for temple stairs and trekking
Light rain gear in the wet season
thingsToDo: Whitewater rafting on the Mae Taeng River
Zipline canopy tours near Mae Rim
Elephant Nature Park ethical sanctuary visit
Chiang Mai Zoo and Night Safari
thingsToAvoid: Booking elephant "riding" camps — choose an ethical sanctuary instead.
Pointing your feet at people, Buddha images, or temple altars.
Visiting in March-April without checking air-quality (seasonal agricultural burning causes regional haze).
topRestaurants: Khao Soi Mae Sai — Khao soi noodle specialist ($)
The Riverside Bar & Restaurant — Thai and international, riverside ($$)
instagramSpots: Wat Phra That Doi Suthep golden pagoda
Old City temple ruins at Wat Chedi Luang
familyActivities: Elephant Nature Park ethical sanctuary visit
Chiang Mai Zoo and Night Safari
adventureActivities: Whitewater rafting on the Mae Taeng River
Zipline canopy tours near Mae Rim
luxuryExperiences: Private hilltop resort stay near Doi Suthep
Private Elephant Nature Park visit with a naturalist guide
Fine dining Northern Thai tasting menu
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset at Wat Phra That Doi Suthep
budgetExperiences: Free Old City temple walking tour
Street food at a local noodle stall
localEtiquette: Dress modestly at temples — shoulders and knees covered, shoes removed before entering.
Never touch anyone's head or point your feet at a person or Buddha image.
The wai (palms-together bow) is the traditional greeting — return it when offered.
localTransportGuide: Songthaews (shared red pickup-truck taxis) are the classic way to get around Chiang Mai cheaply; Grab rideshare is also widely available and often easier for visitors. The Old City itself is compact and walkable or cyclable.
bestTimeToVisit: November to February — the cool, dry season with comfortable temperatures ideal for temple-hopping and trekking.
weather: Tropical climate with three seasons — cool and dry (Nov-Feb, highs around 28°C), hot (Mar-May, highs 35°C+), and rainy (Jun-Oct).
visaInformation: Thailand is visa-free for stays up to 30-60 days for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, depending on nationality and entry method.
averageFlightTime: ~10h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Chiang Mai?
faqs.answer: Thailand is visa-free for stays up to 30-60 days for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, depending on nationality and entry method.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Chiang Mai?
faqs.answer: November to February — the cool, dry season with comfortable temperatures ideal for temple-hopping and trekking. Tropical climate with three seasons — cool and dry (Nov-Feb, highs around 28°C), hot (Mar-May, highs 35°C+), and rainy (Jun-Oct).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Chiang Mai safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Chiang Mai is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Chiang Mai?
faqs.answer: The local currency is THB. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Fernandez
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Chiang Mai Night Safari alone was worth the trip, but combined with Doi Inthanon National Park this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Nadia Ivanov
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Chiang Mai is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Chiang Mai Night Safari. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Lucas Johnson
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Sunday Walking Street market and Elephant Nature Park. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Wei Botha
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. Old City temple walk in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Grace Nguyen
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Doi Inthanon National Park and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Ethan Kowalski
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Nimmanhaemin neighborhood and Old City temple walk. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Sofia Dubois
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Chiang Mai Night Safari in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
